then.verdict: Run the first-90-days list, in order
then.headline: Eleven steps, each with a why — most cost you nothing except remembering
why: SSN first: everything else keys off it.
Credit history starts the day your first card opens — every month you delay is a month of credit age you never get back.
The 401(k) match is a 50–100% instant return; capturing it outranks every investment debate.
The I-94 habit (save it every entry) is the cheapest status-protection there is: the I-94, not the visa stamp, controls your authorized stay.
pros: One afternoon of setup compounds for a decade
cons: Some steps (SSN, state ID) have DMV/SSA queue realities — start early

worst_case.scenario: You skip the I-94 check, an officer typos your admit-until date, and you discover it a year later as an overstay
worst_case.mitigation: Check i94.cbp.dhs.gov after every single entry; errors caught early are fixable at a CBP deferred-inspection office

actions.label: Open checklist C1
actions.how: Money → US first 90 days, with checkboxes that persist

then.verdict: Your India accounts have NRI duties — most people are non-compliant for years
then.headline: NRO conversion, FBAR, and DTAA setup are the three that bite; the rest is hygiene
why: Under FEMA you're supposed to convert resident savings accounts to NRO once you're an NRI — most people never do, and it surfaces at the worst times (property sales, large transfers).
FBAR: if all your non-US accounts COMBINED crossed $10,000 on ANY day of the year, you file FinCEN 114. The penalties for missing it are wildly disproportionate to the crime.
Without a US Tax Residency Certificate (Form 6166) + Form 10F, Indian banks deduct TDS on your NRO interest at the maximum rate instead of the DTAA rate.
Mutual-fund KYC still marked 'resident' quietly breaks redemptions and creates mismatched tax reporting in both countries.
pros: Every item on this list is a one-time or once-a-year fix
cons: Banks make NRO/NRE conversion annoyingly manual
FBAR/8938 are easy to forget in your first US filing season

worst_case.scenario: Years of unreported Indian accounts surface during a green-card or citizenship review, or an FBAR penalty letter arrives
worst_case.mitigation: The IRS has streamlined disclosure procedures for non-willful cases — a cross-border CPA can usually clean up the past at tolerable cost. Start before they find you.
